title: Anisomeris recordii
taxonomy: Anisomeris recordii is a plant species classified within the family Capparaceae. It belongs to the order Brassicales and is a part of the clade known as core eudicots. Further classification places Anisomeris recordii in the clade of angiosperms, specifically within the superorder Rosanae. Its family classification is supported by its fruit structure and leaf morphology. Anisomeris recordii is also known to be native to the Americas, which is a part of its broader distribution and ecological context.

intro: Anisomeris recordii, also known as False unicorn root, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as potential uses in treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Anisomeris recordii include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic properties.
